message: a party was set to be held at his York residence.
had come to the 30th floor of Trump Tower on Fifth Avenue with
a room with a nice view, which had a different kind of awe compared to the director's house in Boston.
If he can afford such an apartment, it clearly means being a director is quite profitable.
When I turned around, Meryl had just come in through the door.
was leaning against the door, barely managing to stand in her loungewear.
You all seemed to have fun while I was away.
Weren't you the one who declined the invitation?
Ariana died, and there’s nothing to be happy about.
No way, barefoot all the way here?
Meryl came at me in a straight line, her eerie laughter echoing.
You bastard! Die! Die! Just die already!
Meryl swung a knife at Guy.
Her repeated attempts, hindered by her unsteady footing and Guy’s quick reflexes, barely grazed him.
Meryl was taken away by the guards, and an abrupt silence fell over the room.
Everyone was left stunned by the sudden turn of events.
Casually glancing down at the floor, I noticed a key at my feet.
Whose lost item could this be?
As I picked up the key and looked up, everyone was already acting as if nothing had happened.
Had it not been for Meryl's interruption, it would have been a perfectly calm party.
However, even with the calm exterior, perhaps each person's heart was swirling with suspicion.
After that, the party dragged on, uneventfully.
lacks the grandeur of the mansion, but everyone seems to be having a good time chatting.
It's lonely not seeing Cindy, Gloria, and Meryl around.
Everyone is laughing and chatting as if nothing happened. I can’t help but think that maybe Meryl was just a dream.
Did what Meryl did stir waves in everyone's hearts?
They seem to be having the time of their lives at the party. However, I hope they don't do anything stupid like they did in Boston.
It seems like they're scheming something regarding Tony,
Seems to be the same as ever, but Meryl’s situation must have shocked him to some degree.
He seems to be enjoying Meryl's incident.
They say the detective's blood might be stirring.
Just don’t cause trouble for those uninvolved.
He gives off an impression of being more easygoing than before. However, I feel like he’s got quite a bit of fatigue built up.
He talks in riddles a lot and remains as dramatic as ever, that Tonny.
He's quietly drinking alone, but I wonder what he’s thinking inside.
He said there’s a common concern shared with the Director,
Does he have something in mind?
Is it because Meryl's not here?
Even though she's worried about Patricia, it seems like her complexion isn't great either. Is it really that serious?
I wonder what she thinks about Meryl barging in.
It seems like she misunderstands Meryl as targeting the director. She had a vibe like she knew something.
He looks like the picture of health; he must have been training every day.
It seems he doesn't know that their mental state was precarious. If it could stay that way without him knowing,
I was worried he might be in shock from that ordeal, but he looks just fine.
He seems shocked to learn about Cindy and Meryl, but if he understands the reason he was attacked, he’ll probably feel better.
Is it really okay to tell Guy that Meryl and Cindy are mother and daughter?
She seems unusually cheery; it must be because Guy has come back.
If we can get through this without any mental issues, that would be the best.
The courage to butt in between Guy and Meryl is impressive. I feel like I've seen the strength of a woman in love.
I was concerned about Ariana's name, but I hope it doesn't trigger any emotional instability.
(She) is staying close to the director. I don't feel good about that.
Even if she is advising the director, despite me saying I like her, she doesn’t pay attention to me at all.
She’s still glued to the director, but it seems like the seriousness between the two of them has increased.
She seems confused about why Meryl attacked Guy,
Are there others who have earned Meryl's resentment that you know of?
I never thought she would really direct her blade at Guy. To come to where everyone is, no less.
It seems Meryl is convinced Guy is the criminal, but the truth is still unclear.
She seems to be quite shaken, but I wonder if she's really okay.
